concerns.text: Phase 4 (US2) tasks T021-T026 are marked as dependent on US1 completion, yet T021-T025 are tagged [P] (parallel-safe). This is a contradiction: if they depend on the output of US1 (ndvi_timeseries.parquet), they cannot be parallel-safe with US1 tasks. The [P] tag implies no shared state or ordering, but the 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section explicitly states US2 depends on US1. Remove [P] from US2 tasks or clarify that [P] only applies to internal US2 parallelism after US1 is done.

Phase 5 (US3) tasks T029-T034 are marked [P] where applicable, but the 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section states US3 depends on US2 completion (requires recovery_trajectories.parquet). Similar to US2, the [P] tags on T029-T032 suggest they can run in parallel with US2, which violates the stated dependency. The [P] tag must be removed from tasks that consume artifacts from a preceding user story.

Task T018 (US1) handles missing revenue data by substituting visitor count proxy (FR-007). However, the logic for 'missing revenue data' implies the existence of a revenue dataset that must be fetched/validated first. There is no explicit task in Phase 3 for fetching or validating the ecotourism revenue/visitor data (only T012 fetches Landsat). T018 consumes an artifact (revenue data) that has no defined producer task in the sequence, creating a 'consumer before producer' gap for the economic data stream.

Task T029 (US3) fetches CHIRPS/MODIS data. While FR-003 mentions controlling for these, there is no explicit task in Phase 2 (Foundational) or Phase 3 (US1) that defines the schema or fetches these covariates. T029 acts as a producer for the modeling step, but it is buried in the final user story. Ideally, covariate acquisition should be a foundational task or part of US1 to ensure data availability before US2/US3 modeling begins, though the current order is technically executable if T029 runs first within US3.
